name: Lord of the Rings
description: Middle-earth, Tolkien's epic, and the journey to Mordor

questions.q: Who is the author of 'The Lord of the Rings'?

source.notes: John Ronald Reuel Tolkien. Professor of Anglo-Saxon at Oxford.

questions.q: In what country was J.R.R. Tolkien born?

source.notes: Born in Bloemfontein, 1892. Moved to England at age 3.

questions.q: The Lord of the Rings is published as three volumes. Which of these is NOT one of them?

source.notes: The Silmarillion is a separate mythology, published 1977 after Tolkien's death.

questions.q: What is the name of Frodo's elderly adventurer uncle?

source.notes: Bilbo Baggins is the hero of 'The Hobbit' (1937).

questions.q: What race are Frodo, Bilbo, Sam, Merry, and Pippin?

source.notes: Also called 'Halflings.' Live in the Shire. Typically 2-4 feet tall.

questions.q: In Bree, the hobbits meet a mysterious ranger. By what nickname does he introduce himself?

source.notes: His true name is Aragorn, heir to the throne of Gondor.

questions.q: Who is the main dark lord and antagonist of 'The Lord of the Rings'?

source.notes: Fallen Maia (angelic being). Forged the One Ring in secret.

questions.q: What is the name of the grey wizard who guides Frodo and the Fellowship?

source.notes: Also known as Mithrandir (Elvish) and Olórin. Returns as Gandalf the White.

questions.q: Who is Gandalf's betrayer, the white wizard who sides with Sauron?

source.notes: Head of the Wizards' Council. Later called 'Sharkey' by his minions.

questions.q: What creature was Gollum before the Ring corrupted him?

source.notes: His original name was Sméagol. Possessed the Ring for ~500 years.

questions.q: Liv Tyler played which elf character in Peter Jackson's film trilogy?

source.notes: Arwen Undómiel, daughter of Elrond. Marries Aragorn.

questions.q: What is the name of the battle fought at Helm's Deep in 'The Two Towers'?

source.notes: Helm's Deep is the name of the fortress. The keep within is called the Hornburg.

questions.q: What special blade stabbed Frodo at Weathertop, requiring elven healing?

source.notes: Wielded by the Witch-king. Shards would turn Frodo into a wraith.

questions.q: 'The Lord of the Rings' was first published in what year?

source.notes: Fellowship published July 1954. Return of the King delayed to October 1955.

questions.q: Whom does Samwise Gamgee marry at the end of the story?

source.notes: They have 13 children together. Sam later becomes Mayor of the Shire 7 times.

questions.q: How many rings of power were originally forged, per the opening poem?

source.notes: 3 for Elves, 7 for Dwarves, 9 for Men, 1 for the Dark Lord (= 20 total including the One Ring).

questions.q: What is the name of the volcano where the One Ring must be destroyed?

source.notes: Mount Doom is the common name; Orodruin is its Sindarin name.

questions.q: How many members make up the original Fellowship of the Ring?

source.notes: 4 hobbits, 1 wizard, 2 men, 1 elf, 1 dwarf = 9. One for each of the Nazgûl.

questions.q: What is the name of the giant spider that attacks Frodo in the tunnels of Cirith Ungol?

source.notes: Descendant of Ungoliant. Samwise defeats her with his Elvish sword Sting.

questions.q: The film 'The Return of the King' won how many Academy Awards, tying a record?

source.notes: 11 wins in 2004, tying 'Ben-Hur' (1959) and 'Titanic' (1997). Won all 11 categories it was nominated in.
